title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
summary: Aluminum prices fell up to 2.7% on the London Metal Exchange as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump plans to roll back some levies on aluminum and steel products due to concerns over consumer impact. The metal traded 2.3% lower at $3,030.00 per ton as of 1:03 p.m. local time amid a broad cross-asset selloff.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights Hydro's HalZero technology, which eliminates CO2 emissions during aluminum smelting by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop. It also covers innovations in removing metal impurities from recycled aluminum and Rio Tinto's use of hydrogen to replace natural gas in alumina refining.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that captures over 99% of aluminum ions from hazardous waste streams in aluminum production. The captured aluminum can be upcycled into bulk production, increasing yield while significantly reducing waste.

title: Aluminum Recycling: Innovation & Sustainability
summary: TOMRA explores how cutting-edge technologies like Dynamic LIBS and deep learning are transforming aluminum recycling by enhancing material purity and sustainability. These innovations are fueling the green revolution in the aluminum industry.

title: US aluminum, steel prices jump on Trump's new tariffs
summary: US steel and aluminum prices soared on Monday after President Donald Trump announced he would double tariffs on the two metals to 50%, intensifying the global trade war. The new tariffs are set to take effect on June 4th, causing shares of foreign steelmakers to slump.

title: American Steel Just Got More Expensive. Buyers Blame Tariffs.
summary: Cleveland-Cliffs sold steel for an average price of $1,015 a ton in the second quarter, up from $980 in the first quarter, as manufacturers pass additional expenses onto domestic producers. These price hikes are a direct result of tariffs imposed on steel and aluminum by President Trump.

title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: Indonesia's strategic shift represents the most significant development in nickel markets, driving a critical inflection point as supply and demand dynamics converge. The global market is moving beyond the prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton that persisted since April 2025[2].

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has notified PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, to drastically cut its ore output from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons this year to boost global prices. The company plans to apply for a revision of this quota, while nickel prices recently climbed following Indonesia's announcement of planned output cuts.

title: Nickel price spikes as risks to supply grow in top producer Indonesia
summary: Nickel prices rose 1.3% to $18,806 a ton on the LME after reports that 10-15% of high-grade nickel pig iron capacity at Indonesia's Weda Bay Industrial Park will undergo rotational maintenance. This supply risk in the top producer has triggered a sharp rally in futures during Asian trading hours.

title: Dazi Usa sull'acciaio, la Cogne Acciai Speciali attende: il mercato americano pesa per l'8-10%
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali, storica acciaieria di Aosta, si dice 'attendista' di fronte ai nuovi dazi del 25% annunciati da Donald Trump sulle importazioni di acciaio e alluminio a partire dal 12 marzo.
